extra.message: Unsanitized input from an HTTP parameter flows into the return value of fetch, where it is used to render an HTML page returned to the user. This may result in a Cross-Site Scripting attack (XSS).
Avoid hardcoding values that are meant to be secret. Found a hardcoded string used in here.
Unsanitized input from the request URL flows into open, where it is used as a path. This may result in a Path Traversal vulnerability and allow an attacker to read arbitrary files.
Unsanitized input from an HTTP parameter flows into urllib.request.urlopen, where it is used as an URL to perform a request. This may result in a Server Side Request Forgery vulnerability.
Unsanitized input from a command line argument flows into get, where it is used as an URL to perform a request. This may result in a Server Side Request Forgery vulnerability.
Unsanitized input from a command line argument flows into get, where it is used as an URL to perform a request. This may result in a Server Side Request Forgery vulnerability.
Unsanitized input from a command line argument flows into post, where it is used as an URL to perform a request. This may result in a Server Side Request Forgery vulnerability.
Unsanitized input from a command line argument flows into post, where it is used as an URL to perform a request. This may result in a Server Side Request Forgery vulnerability.
Unsanitized input from a command line argument flows into post, where it is used as an URL to perform a request. This may result in a Server Side Request Forgery vulnerability.
Unsanitized input from a command line argument flows into post, where it is used as an URL to perform a request. This may result in a Server Side Request Forgery vulnerability.
Do not hardcode credentials in code. Found hardcoded credential used in here.
